title: quercus × leana
intro: Quercus × leana, also known as the Leana oak, is a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Quercus robur (English oak) and Quercus petraea (Sessile oak). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high tannin content, which has been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Quercus × leana include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. Quercus × leana is often prepared for herbal use by infusing its leaves in hot water to make a tea or by drying and crushing its leaves to make a tincture or infusion.
